entries: The monadic deva speaks a word to cause a creature's soul to recoil at its sins or an undead creature to recoil at its lack of a soul. One non-good living or undead target within 40 feet takes {@damage 5d10} good damage and must attempt a {@dc 32} Fortitude save.
Regardless of the outcome, the target is then temporarily immune for 10 minutes.

entries.Success: The target takes half damage and is {@condition stunned 1}.
entries.Failure: The target takes full damage and is {@condition stunned 2}.
entries.Critical Failure: The target takes double damage and is {@condition stunned 4}.

bot.name: Rebuke Soul

entries: When a monadic deva hits a target for the second time during its turn with its mace, it deals an additional {@damage 2d12} force damage as its weapon shimmers with ripples of power.
bot.name: Solid Blow

entries: 50 feet. The moonflower releases a pulse of bright light. Each non-moonflower creature in the emanation must attempt a {@dc 23} Fortitude save. The moonflower can't use Light Pulse again for {@dice 1d4} rounds.

entries.Success: The creature is unaffected.
entries.Failure: The creature is {@condition dazzled} for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
entries.Critical Failure: The creature is {@condition blinded} for {@dice 1d4} rounds.

bot.name: Light Pulse

bot.trigger: The moonflower has swallowed a creature
entries: The swallowed creature is wrapped in a tight cocoon and extruded from the moonflower's body into an adjacent square. The creature continues to be Swallowed Whole. It can't use {@skill Acrobatics} to {@action Escape} a pod, but other creatures can attempt to Rupture the pod. The cocooned creature takes half damage from any damage dealt to the cocoon. Once the cocoon is Ruptured, it deflates and decays.
bot.name: Pod Prison

entries: Should a Small or larger creature die within a pod prison, the pod transforms into an adult moonflower with full Hit Points after {@dice 1d4} hours of growth. The newly formed moonflower has its own consciousness, but some aspect of its trunk or blossoms resembles the creature that died within. The dead creature's equipment remains inside the new moonflower and can be retrieved if the moonflower is slain.
bot.name: Pod Spawn

entries: Large, {@damage 2d10+10} bludgeoning and {@damage 2d6} acid, Rupture 21.
bot.name: Swallow Whole

entries: The morlock tinkers with an adjacent construct or mechanical hazard. They attempt a {@skill Crafting} check against the construct's or hazard's Fortitude DC. The morlock can't succeed if the target's level is more than double the morlock's.

entries.Critical Success: The target gains {@dice 4d6} HP and a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls for 1 minute.
entries.Success: The target gains {@dice 2d6} HP.
entries.Critical Failure: The morlock injures itself, taking {@dice 2d6} damage (typically bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing, but potentially a different type at the GM's discretion).

bot.name: Instinctual Tinker

entries: The morlock {@action Stride||Strides} up to twice its Speed, during which it attempts a {@action High Jump} or a {@action Long Jump}. At any point during its movement, the morlock can make a melee {@action Strike} against an enemy in its reach. The morlock then can't use {@action Leap} Attack for 1 round.
bot.name: Leap Attack

entries: A morlock's {@action Strike||Strikes} deal an extra {@damage 1d6} precision damage to {@condition flat-footed} creatures.
bot.name: Sneak Attack

entries: A morlock can share the same space as another morlock, but no more than two morlocks can occupy the same space. When morlocks share the same space, they gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls.
bot.name: Swarming Stance

entries: 20 feet. Allies in the movanic deva's aura gain a +1 status bonus to all saving throws, resistance 10 to positive and negative damage, and are unharmed by the effects of a plane's {@trait positive} and {@trait negative} traits. Animals in the aura of 12th level or lower don't attack the movanic deva or the deva's allies unless they are {@condition controlled} or otherwise forced to attack.
mid.name: Aura of Vitality

entries: The movanic deva attempts to unravel unwelcome magic effects on allies within its aura of vitality to protect them from malevolent forces. The movanic deva attempts a counteract check against as many spell effects affecting allies in the area as it wishes with a +19 counteract modifier and a counteract level of 4, rolling once for the selected effects.
bot.name: Dispelling Field

entries: When a movanic deva wields a weapon, that weapon gains the effect of a flaming rune.
bot.name: Flaming Armaments

entries: 10 feet. The ground in the area is {@quickref difficult terrain||3|terrain} for all non-mudwretch creatures.
top.name: Muddy Field

entries: If a mudwretch takes any damage from horrid wilting or a similar effect, takes 10 or more fire damage from a single effect, or spends more than 24 hours outside of a source of sufficient hydration (such as a swamp, river, well, or recent rainfall), it becomes dehydrated. While dehydrated, the mudwretch can't Spew Mud, is {@condition sickened 2}, and is {@condition slowed 1} until it either fully immerses in water, spends 1 minute in the rain, or rehydrates in another way (such as via Gory Hydration).
mid.name: Susceptible to Desiccation

entries: {@damage 1d8+2} bludgeoning, {@dc 18}
bot.name: Constrict

bot.trigger: The mudwretch deals Constrict damage to a living creature that has blood
bot.requirements: The mudwretch is dehydrated
entries: The mudwretch squeezes harder, dealing {@damage 1d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed damage} to the target. The mudwretch absorbs this blood, removing any penalties it had as a result of being dehydrated.
bot.name: Gory Hydration

entries: Until it next acts, the mudwretch appears to be an ordinary puddle of mud.
It has an automatic result of 20 on {@skill Deception} checks to pass as a mud puddle and can make a fist {@action Strike} against a creature that walks onto the mud puddle as a reaction.
bot.name: Mud Puddle

entries: The mudwretch spews a 20-foot line of pressurized mud that deals {@damage 2d10} bludgeoning damage ({@dc 18} basic Reflex save). On a critical failure, a creature also takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds for 1 round. The mudwretch can't Spew Mud again for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
bot.name: Spew Mud

entries: 30 feet. When a non-demon ends its turn in the aura, it must attempt a {@dc 23} Fortitude save. If it fails, it becomes {@condition drained 1}.
mid.name: Death-Stealing Gaze

entries: A nabasu denied food suffers painful backlash as their demonic nature feeds on their own corrupted spirituality. If a nabasu's Consume Death is disrupted (such as by a fighter using Disruptive Stance) or a creature resists the effect with a critical success, the nabasu takes {@damage 4d6} mental damage.
mid.name: Starvation Vulnerability

entries: The nabasu focuses their death-stealing gaze upon a single target they can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Fortitude save against death-stealing gaze.

entries.Critical Success: The creature is unaffected and the nabasu suffers starvation vulnerability.
entries.Success: The creature is unaffected.
entries.Failure: The creature is affected by deathstealing gaze and becomes {@condition drained 1}. If the creature was already {@condition drained 1} by the death-stealing gaze before attempting the save, a failed save increases the value of the {@condition drained} condition by 1, to a maximum of {@condition drained 4}. The nabasu gains 10 temporary Hit Points, and the {@condition drained} creature is temporarily immune until the start of the nabasu's next turn.
entries.Critical Failure: As failure, but the creature increases the amount of drain by 2.

bot.name: Consume Death

entries: A creature that dies while suffering drain from a nabasu's death-stealing gaze rises as a ghoul (Pathfinder Bestiary 168) the next midnight.
bot.name: Stolen Death

entries: A nalfeshnee's greed is such that losing possessions causes them harm. If an item is stolen from a nalfeshnee, the demon takes {@damage 3d6+10} mental damage.
mid.name: Forfeiture Aversion

mid.trigger: A creature critically fails a weapon {@action Strike} against the nalfeshnee
entries: The nalfeshnee attempts to {@action Disarm} the weapon used in the triggering {@action Strike} at a –2 circumstance penalty. On a success, the nalfeshnee steals the weapon.
mid.name: Greedy Grab

entries: The nalfeshnee steals all unattended items glowing with its Light of Avarice into an extradimensional space. The demon can {@action Interact} to regurgitate any number of these items into their hand or onto the ground. If the demon dies, is affected by a {@trait teleportation} effect, or consumes an extradimensional space (such as a {@item bag of holding (generic)}), they vomit up all the items.
bot.name: Claim Wealth

entries: Beams of unholy light shoot from the nalfeshnee toward four items within 60 feet. If someone is holding or wearing a targeted item, they can keep it from being affected with a successful {@dc 34} Reflex save. For 1 minute, the affected items glow in nauseating colors. Any non-demon is {@condition sickened 2} and {@condition slowed 1} as long as it holds, wears, or touches a glowing item. Recovering from the sickness requires a successful {@dc 29} Will save instead of a Fortitude save. Ending the sickness this way ends the {@condition slowed} condition and makes the creature temporarily immune to Light of Avarice for 24 hours. If the creature removes or drops the item, both conditions end immediately but the creature doesn't become immune to Light of Avarice.
bot.name: Light of Avarice

abilities: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

entries: The necrophidius sways, its serpentine form undulating and clattering in a hypnotic rhythm. Each creature that witnesses the dance must attempt a {@dc 18} Will save.

entries.Critical Success: The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
entries.Success: The creature is unnerved by the swaying and becomes {@condition flat-footed} until the end of the necrophidius's next turn.
entries.Failure: The creature is distracted by the swaying, becoming {@condition stunned 1}. After it recovers, it is {@condition flat-footed} until the end of the necrophidius's next turn.
entries.Critical Failure: As failure, but {@condition stunned 3}.

bot.name: Dance of Death

entries: A living creature bitten by a necrophidius must succeed at a {@dc 20} Fortitude save or become {@condition paralyzed}. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 for each save attempted. A creature that succeeds at this save is temporarily immune to necrophidic paralysis for 24 hours.
bot.name: Necrophidic Paralysis

entries: The neothelid uses its mind as a precise sense at the listed range to notice all non-mindless creatures.
top.name: Thoughtsense

mid.trigger: A creature the neothelid is aware of uses a {@trait teleportation} effect
entries: The neothelid creates an extradimensional wormhole at the triggering creature's initial location that persists for up to 1 minute.
If the neothelid moves into the wormhole's space, the neothelid teleports to the same destination as the triggering creature.
mid.name: Dimensional Wormhole

entries: The neothelid breathes a spray of acid that deals {@damage 16d6} acid damage in a 60-foot cone ({@dc 38} basic Reflex save). The neothelid can't use Breath Weapon again for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
bot.name: Breath Weapon

bot.trigger: A creature fails a Will save against a spell or effect created by the neothelid
entries: The neothelid casts a {@spell suggestion} spell it has available on the creature that failed its Will save.
bot.name: Instant Suggestion

entries: The neothelid's four tongues lash out in a frenzy at several targets. It makes up to four rasping tongue {@action Strike||Strikes}, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the neothelid's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the neothelid makes all of its attacks.
bot.name: Lashing Tongues

entries: Large, {@damage 2d12} bludgeoning plus {@damage 2d12} acid, Rupture 27.
bot.name: Swallow Whole

entries: When underwater, the nereid's body is {@condition invisible}. The nereid can dismiss or resume this transparency as an action that has the {@trait concentrate} trait.
mid.name: Watery Transparency

entries: The nereid touches a creature and causes water from its own body to flow into the creature's lungs. If the creature cannot breathe water, it must attempt a {@dc 29} Fortitude save.

entries.Success: The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Drowning Touch for 24 hours.
entries.Failure: The creature becomes {@condition sickened 3} as it chokes on the water.
entries.Critical Failure: The creature chokes on the water and runs out of air. It falls {@condition unconscious} and starts drowning (Pathfinder Core Rulebook 478). If the creature is above water, it recovers from drowning as soon as it succeeds at a saving throw against suffocation.

bot.name: Drowning Touch

entries: The nereid divests themself of part of their connection to the First World and imbues this essence into a flowing shawl that enables them to function on land. The nereid can {@action Dismiss} this effect as long as they are touching the shawl. As long as the shawl exists, the nereid gains the {@trait amphibious} trait. A nonnereid who carries the shawl also gains the {@trait amphibious} trait. If a nereid's shawl is destroyed rather than Dismissed, the nereid can't Manifest a Shawl for 24 hours.
bot.name: Manifest Shawl

entries: The nixie can use {@skill Diplomacy} to {@action Make an Impression} on and make very simple Requests of aquatic or amphibious animals.
top.name: Wild Empathy

entries: The nixie can duplicate any 1st-level spell or produce any effect with a power level in line with a 1st-level spell, but only in response to the request or desire of a non-fey creature. The creature whose desire is granted can never again benefit from that particular nixie's Grant Desire ability.
bot.name: Grant Desire

attacks.name: norn shears
attacks.damage: {@damage 4d6+15} slashing plus {@damage 5d6} negative and sever fate

entries: A norn automatically rolls a 20 when she rolls initiative.
top.name: Sense Fate

entries: This functions as the {@ability coven} ability, except only norns can join a triumvirate, and it functions only as long as exactly three norns are part of the triumvirate. A triumvirate grants the following spells: {@spell alter reality} (once per day), {@spell cataclysm}, {@spell discern location}, {@spell foresight}, and {@spell revival}.
top.name: Triumvirate

entries: When a creature is subject to a {@trait fortune} effect from a norn and a {@trait misfortune} effect from any source other than a norn (or vice versa), the norn's effect automatically counteracts the other effect and then takes place normally, rather than the two effects canceling each other out. If both the {@trait fortune} and {@trait misfortune} effect are from a norn, then the two cancel each other out as normal. At the GM's discretion, powerful entities related to fate or luck, like Desna, Magdh, or Pharasma, count as a norn for the purpose of this ability.
bot.name: Fated

entries: When a norn deals negative damage with a {@action Strike}, she regains 10 Hit Points. The target must succeed at a {@dc 39} Fortitude save or become {@condition drained 1} ({@condition drained 2} on a critical failure). Further negative damage dealt by the norn increases the {@condition drained} condition value by 1 on a failed save (or by 2 on a critical failure), to a maximum of {@condition drained 4}.
bot.name: Sever Fate

bot.trigger: A creature within 120 feet attempts a saving throw
entries: The creature rolls the saving throw twice, and then the norn decides which result applies. If the norn chooses the lower roll, this is a {@trait misfortune} effect; if she chooses the higher roll, it's a {@trait fortune} effect; if they're the same, she decides which trait to apply.
bot.name: Shift Fate

entries: The norn produces a golden thread linked to the fate of a creature within 100 feet of her, then snips it short with her shears. The target takes 100 negative damage ({@dc 42} basic Fortitude save). If the target is reduced to 0 Hit Points from this damage, the thread is completely severed and the creature dies immediately. A creature slain by Snip Thread can't be restored to life except by miracle, wish, or similarly powerful magic; or by divine intervention. Regardless of the outcome of their saving throw, a creature targeted by Snip Thread then becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours. The norn can't use Snip Thread again for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
bot.name: Snip Thread

entries: 30 feet, {@dc 25}
mid.name: Frightful Presence

entries: Unpolluted fresh water burns a nuckelavee like acid, dealing {@dice 1d6} damage to it and causing it to be {@condition sickened 2}. A nuckelavee can't heal from damage when it's in an area that isn't polluted (subject to GM discretion).
mid.name: Purity Vulnerability

entries: The nuckelavee breathes a 30-foot cone of foulness, dealing {@damage 8d6} negative damage ({@dc 28} basic Fortitude save) to living creatures in the area.
A creature that fails also takes {@damage 2d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed damage}.
The nuckelavee can't use Breath Weapon again for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
bot.name: Breath Weapon
Mortasheen
bot.note: The target can't recover from the {@condition fatigued} condition caused by mortasheen until the disease is cured. Mortasheen gains the {@trait virulent} trait against animals and plants

entries: Medium or smaller, hoof, {@dc 28}
bot.name: Trample

mid.trigger: A Medium creature within the nuglub's reach leaves a square during its move action
entries: The nuglub lashes out at the triggering creature's knees and tries to knock them {@condition prone}. The nuglub makes an {@skill Acrobatics} check against the creature's Reflex DC. On a success, the target falls and lands {@condition prone}.
mid.name: Kneecapper

entries: A nuglub's {@action Strike||Strikes} deal an additional {@damage 1d6} precision damage to {@condition flat-footed} targets, or {@dice 1d10} if the target is {@condition prone}.
bot.name: Sneak Attack

attacks.name: jaws
attacks.damage: {@damage 2d6+13} piercing plus {@damage 2d6} acid, {@damage 1d6} chaotic, and Grab

attacks.name: tentacle mouth
attacks.damage: {@damage 2d6+13} piercing plus {@damage 1d6} acid, {@damage 1d6} chaotic, and Grab

mid.trigger: The nyogoth takes piercing or slashing damage
entries: The nyogoth sprays its acidic blood on adjacent creatures, dealing {@damage 6d6} acid damage ({@dc 29} basic Reflex save).
mid.name: Caustic Blood

bot.requirements: The nyogoth has {@condition grabbed} a creature
entries: The nyogoth slavers and chews at the {@condition grabbed} creature, dealing {@damage 2d6+7} slashing and {@damage 1d6} acid damage ({@dc 29} basic Fortitude save).
bot.name: Feeding Frenzy

entries: The nyogoth untangles its tentacles and prolapses its many mouths, turning itself inside out in a truly nauseating display.
Creatures in a 30-foot emanation must attempt a {@dc 29} Will save, after which they are temporarily immune to further Nauseating Displays for 1 minute.

entries.Success: The creature is {@condition sickened 1}.
entries.Failure: The creature is {@condition stunned 3} and {@condition sickened 1}.
entries.Critical Failure: The creature is {@condition stunned 5} and {@condition sickened 2}.

bot.name: Nauseating Display

entries: The ogre spider has imprecise {@ability tremorsense} to detect the vibrations of creatures touching its web.
top.name: Web Sense

entries: An ogre spider can fit through tight spaces as if it were a Large creature. While {@action Squeeze||Squeezing}, it can move at its full speed.
bot.name: Eerie Flexibility

entries: A creature hit by the ogre spider's web attack is {@condition immobilized} and stuck to the nearest surface until it {@action Escape||Escapes} ({@dc 22}).
bot.name: Web Trap

entries: 60 feet. Any creature that begins its turn in the aura is {@condition doomed 1} for as long as it remains in the aura and for 1 hour thereafter.
mid.name: Aura of Doom

entries: The daemon expels a shrieking black cloud of smoke from its mouth in a 120-foot line or a 60-foot cone
(or both a line and a cone in the same direction if it spends 3 actions). Living creatures in the area take {@damage 24d6} negative damage ({@dc 45} basic Reflex save)
The olethrodaemon can't use Apocalypse Breath again for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
bot.name: Apocalypse Breath

bot.requirements: The olethrodaemon has a soul gem
entries: The olethrodaemon crushes the soul gem in one hand and regains the use of Apocalypse Breath or any one of its innate spells.
bot.name: Soul Crush

entries: Huge, {@damage 4d10+10} bludgeoning and {@condition drained 2}, Rupture 43. An olethrodaemon has numerous stomachs connected by labyrinthine digestive organs. Swallowed creatures cannot {@action Escape}, but can attempt to cut their way out with a {@dice 1d6} roll. On a roll of 1–3, the creature cuts into yet another stomach.
On a roll of 4–6, the creature manages to cut its way out of the olethrodaemon.
bot.name: Swallow Whole

entries: The onidoshi takes on the appearance of an {@creature ogre}. This doesn't change their Speed or {@action Strike} attack and damage.
bot.name: Change Shape

entries: The ooze mephit spits toxic slime in a 15-foot cone that deals {@damage 2d6} poison damage to each creature within the area ({@dc 17} basic Reflex save). The ooze mephit can't use Breath Weapon again for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
bot.name: Breath Weapon

abilities: fast healing 2 (while touching mud or slime)

entries: An orca can use its hearing as a precise sense at the listed range, but only underwater.
top.name: Aquatic Echolocation

entries: An orca can hold its breath for 2 hours.
top.name: Deep Breath

entries: 30 feet. An orca can travel no further than 5 feet onto land as part of an Aquatic Ambush. After it does so, it is {@condition prone} until it Crawls to return to the water.
bot.name: Aquatic Ambush

entries: The orca Swims up to its swim Speed, then Leaps vertically out of the water up to 25 feet in the air, making a {@action Strike} against a creature at any point during the jump (this lets it attack a creature within 30 feet of the water's surface). After the {@action Strike}, the orca splashes back down into the water.
bot.name: Breach

entries: The guard makes a melee {@action Strike}. This counts as two attacks when calculating the guard's multiple attack penalty. If this {@action Strike} hits, the guard deals an extra die of weapon damage.
bot.name: Power Attack

entries: A velstrac automatically knows whether a creature it sees has any of the {@condition doomed}, {@condition dying}, and {@condition wounded} conditions, as well as the value of those conditions.
top.name: Painsight

entries: The ostiarius always knows the direction and distance to the closest portal between the Shadow.
top.name: Sense Portal

entries: This sense functions only on these two planes.
top.name: Plane and the Material Plane.

entries: 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it hears the wounds on the ostiarius's body whisper obscene truths. The creature must succeed at a {@dc 21} Will save or become {@condition sickened 1}.
mid.name: Whispering Wounds

entries: The ostiarius inspires their willing allies and themself by whispering words of courage from their wounds.
The ostiarius and their allies in a 50-foot emanation gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, and saves against {@trait fear} effects.

The ostiarius can Sustain Compel Courage. Non-velstracs who accept this compelled courage find bleeding wounds opening on their own bodies to whisper in thanks. They take 1 {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed damage} and can't attempt a flat check to end this damage as long as they're compelled.
bot.name: Compel Courage

entries: The ostiarius stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The creature must immediately attempt a Will save against whispering wounds. In addition, if the creature was already {@condition sickened} and fails its save, the creature is {@condition fascinated} by the ostiarius and can't use {@condition hostile} actions. This fascination lasts for 1 round or until the ostiarius takes any {@condition hostile} action against the creature or the creature's allies. Whether the creature succeeds at or fails the save, it is temporarily immune for 1 hour.
bot.name: Focus Gaze

attacks.name: stinger
attacks.damage: {@damage 1d10+11} piercing plus {@damage 1d6} evil and osyluth venom

bot.name: Osyluth Venom
entries: Once a creature reaches stage 3, it takes a –4 status penalty to Will saves against attempts to {@action Coerce} it for 1 hour (this is a {@trait mental} effect).
The osyluth can cast innate {@spell invisibility} using only 1 action.
bot.name: Quick Invisibility

entries: An osyluth deals an extra {@dice 2d6} damage whenever they {@action Strike} an {@condition enfeebled}, {@condition frightened}, or {@condition prone} creature.
bot.name: Sadistic Strike

entries: The osyluth telepathically questions a creature affected by {@spell zone of truth}. After 1 round, if the creature intentionally refuses to answer the question, it takes {@damage 2d6} mental damage.
bot.name: Stygian Inquisitor

entries: The osyluth sweeps their tail in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the cone must succeed at a {@dc 26} Reflex save or be knocked {@condition prone}.
bot.name: Tail Sweep

abilities: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

entries: The pachycephalosaurus {@action Stride||Strides} up to its Speed. If it ends its movement within melee reach of a target, it can make a skull {@action Strike} against that target. If the pachycephalosaurus critically hits with this {@action Strike}, the creature hit is {@condition stunned 1}.
bot.name: Clobbering Charge

bot.trigger: The pachycephalosaurus damages a Medium or smaller foe with its skull {@action Strike}
entries: The pachycephalosaurus digs in and flings its head up, shoving its foe away. It attempts an {@skill Athletics} check against the target's Fortitude DC.

entries.Critical Success: The pachycephalosaurus pushes the opponent up to 10 feet away from itself and knocks the target {@condition prone}.
entries.Success: The pachycephalosaurus pushes the opponent back 5 feet.
entries.Failure: The pachycephalosaurus fails to push the opponent.
entries.Critical Failure: As failure, but the failed attempt leaves the pachycephalosaurus {@condition flat-footed} for 1 round.

bot.name: Sudden Shove

entries: If the peluda takes 30 or more slashing damage from a critical hit, the attacker severs the peluda's tail. The peluda takes {@damage 2d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed damage} and can't make tail attacks until their tail grows back (in about 1 week).
mid.name: Vulnerable Tail

mid.trigger: A creature within 10 feet attempts a melee {@action Strike} against the peluda
entries: The peluda shifts their position and makes a quill {@action Strike} against the attacking creature. This {@action Strike} doesn't count toward the peluda's multiple attack penalty, and the peluda's multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this {@action Strike}.
mid.name: Quill Thrust

entries: The peluda breathes a torrent of flames that deals {@damage 7d10} fire damage in a 60-foot line ({@dc 29} basic Reflex save). They can't use their Breath Weapon again for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
bot.name: Breath Weapon

entries: The peluda bristles their quills and shakes, sending dozens of spear-like barbs in every direction. All creatures within 30 feet take {@damage 11d6} piercing damage ({@dc 29} basic Reflex save) and are exposed to peluda venom if they take any damage. The peluda can't use Quill Barrage again for 1 minute.
bot.name: Quill Barrage

entries: The peryton rips out the heart of an adjacent corpse with their jaws.

The creature must have died in the last minute. As the peryton rips the heart free and swallows it whole, they regain {@dice 2d6} HP, and any non-peryton that witnesses this event must succeed at a {@dc 21} Will save or become {@condition frightened 1} (or {@condition frightened 2} on a critical failure).
bot.name: Heart Ripper

bot.requirements: The target must be casting a shadow
entries: The peryton Flies, going no higher than 20 feet over the target creature. The target creature must succeed at a {@dc 21} Will save or the peryton's shadow changes to match that cast by the target creature. With their shadow so transformed, the peryton gains a +2 status bonus to attack and damage rolls against that creature. In addition, each time the peryton successfully {@action Strike||Strikes} that creature, the creature must succeed at a {@dc 21} Will save or become {@condition frightened 1}, or increase its {@condition frightened} condition by 1 if it's already {@condition frightened}. This is an {@trait emotion} and {@trait fear} effect. The shadow remains transformed for 1 hour or until the peryton Mimics a Shadow again, whichever comes first.
bot.name: Mimic Shadow

raw.name: Petitioner (Abaddon)
raw.description: The hunted appear as Medium sized versions of themselves from their prior lives, and they exist to be pursued and consumed by daemons.

raw.name: Petitioner (Abyss)
raw.description: The larvae appear as maggot-like grubs with the face the petitioners had in life.

raw.name: Petitioner (Axis)
raw.description: The unmade appear as parchment-skinned humanoids covered in lines of close black script.

raw.name: Petitioner (Astral Plane)
raw.description: The untethered appear as astrally projected versions of their mortal forms, yet they lack a silver cord.

notes: any one spoken in life (such as {@language common})

raw.name: Petitioner (Boneyard)
raw.description: The dead appear as animated skeletons of the type of creature they were when they were alive.

raw.name: Petitioner (Dead Vault)
raw.description: The caged are sent to Rovagug's prison plane, the Dead Vault; they appear as chained versions of their mortal bodies, save that their faces are hideous insectile visages.

entries: bite
bot.name: Rend

entries: All petitioners are formed from and personify the nature of the plane on which they manifest, and their statistics are adjusted as summarized below. They also gain any trait associated with creatures from their plane.

items: {@b {@creature Petitioner (Abaddon)|B2|Abaddon} (the Hunted)} The hunted appear as Medium sized versions of themselves from their prior lives, and they exist to be pursued and consumed by daemons; {@b Alignment} {@trait NE}; {@b Language} {@language Daemonic}; {@b Additional Ability} {@ability fast healing} 5; {@b Melee} fist {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} bludgeoning

{@b {@creature Petitioner (Abyss)|B2|Abyss} (the Larvae)} The larvae appear as maggot-like grubs with the face the petitioners had in life; {@b Alignment} {@trait CE}; {@b Language} {@language Abyssal}; {@b Additional Ability} immune to disease and poison; {@b Melee} jaws {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} piercing

{@b {@creature Petitioner (Astral Plane)|B2|Astral Plane} (the Untethered)} The untethered appear as astrally projected versions of their mortal forms, yet they lack a silver cord; {@b Alignment} any; {@b Language} any one spoken in life (such as {@language Common}); {@b Additional Ability} fly Speed 20 feet; {@b Melee} fist {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} bludgeoning

{@b {@creature Petitioner (Axis)|B2|Axis} (the Unmade)} The unmade appear as parchment-skinned humanoids covered in lines of close black script; {@b Alignment} {@trait LN}; {@b Language} {@language Utopian}; {@b Additional Ability} immune to emotion and fear; {@b Melee} fist {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} bludgeoning

{@b {@creature Petitioner (Boneyard)|B2|Boneyard} (the Dead)} The dead appear as animated skeletons of the type of creature they were when they were alive; {@b Alignment} {@trait N}; {@b Language} {@language Requian}; {@b Additional Ability} resist piercing and slashing 3; {@b Melee} claw {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} slashing

{@b {@creature Petitioner (Dead Vault)|B2|Dead Vault} (the Caged)} The caged are sent to Rovagug's prison plane, the Dead Vault; they appear as chained versions of their mortal bodies, save that their faces are hideous insectile visages; {@b Alignment} {@trait CE}; {@b Language} {@language Abyssal}; {@b Additional Ability} Rend [one-action] bite; {@b Melee} bite {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} piercing

{@b Dimension of Dreams (the Dreamers)} The dreamers appear as their ideal appearance, age, and gender from their mortal bodies; {@b Alignment} any; {@b Language} {@language Aklo}; {@b Additional Ability} {@dice 1d20+1|+1} circumstance bonus on all saving throws; {@b Melee} fist {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} bludgeoning

{@b Elysium (the Chosen)} The chosen appear as idealized versions of their mortal selves with glowing eyes; {@b Alignment} {@trait CG}; {@b Language} {@language Celestial}; {@b Additional Ability} immune to blindness and dazzled; {@b Melee} fist {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} bludgeoning

{@b Ethereal Plane (the Terrorized)} The terrorized appear as sickly, haunted-looking versions of their mortal selves; {@b Alignment} any; {@b Language} any one spoken in life (such as {@language Common}); {@b Additional Ability} immune to bleed and death effects; {@b Melee} fist {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} bludgeoning

{@b Heaven (the Elect)} The elect have golden halos and ghostly wings, but they otherwise appear as their mortal forms; {@b Alignment} {@trait LG}; {@b Language} {@language Celestial}; {@b Additional Ability} fly Speed 20 feet; {@b Melee} wing {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} bludgeoning

{@b Hell (the Damned)} The damned look like terribly scarred or mutilated versions of their mortal selves; {@b Alignment} {@trait LE}; {@b Language} {@language Infernal}; {@b Additional Ability} immune to fire; {@b Melee} jaws {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} piercing

{@b Maelstrom (the Shapeless)} The shapeless appear as half-melted reflections of their former mortal selves; {@b Alignment} {@trait CN}; {@b Language} {@language Protean}; {@b Additional Ability} immune to critical hits and precision; {@b Melee} fist {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} bludgeoning

{@b Material Plane (the Remnants)} The remnants are among the rarest of petitioners; they appear as plain, bland versions of the ancestry of their mortal lives; {@b Alignment} any; {@b Language} any one spoken in life (such as {@language Common}); {@b Additional Ability} ferocity; {@b Melee} fist {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} bludgeoning

{@b Nirvana (the Cleansed)} The cleansed take on animal forms that match their personalities in life; {@b Alignment} {@trait NG}; {@b Language} {@language Celestial}; {@b Additional Ability} speed 35 feet; {@b Melee} claw {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} slashing

{@b Plane of Air (the Air Pneuma)} Air pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves composed of air; {@b Alignment} any; {@b Language} {@language Auran}; {@b Additional Ability} fly Speed 20 feet; {@b Melee} gust {@dice 1d20+7|+7} ({@trait air}), Damage {@dice 1d8+2} bludgeoning

{@b Plane of Earth (the Earth Pneuma)} Earth pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves built of earth or stone; {@b Alignment} any; {@b Language} {@language Terran}; {@b Additional Ability} burrow Speed 20 feet; {@b Melee} fist {@dice 1d20+7|+7} ({@trait earth}), Damage {@dice 1d8+2} bludgeoning

{@b Plane of Fire (the Fire Pneuma)} Fire pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves shaped from flame; {@b Alignment} any; {@b Language} {@language Ignan}; {@b Additional Ability} immunity to fire; {@b Melee} cinder {@dice 1d20+7|+7} ({@trait fire}), Damage {@dice 1d8+2} fire

{@b Plane of Water (the Water Pneuma)} Water pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves formed of flowing water; {@b Alignment} any; {@b Language} {@language Aquan}; {@b Additional Ability} {@ability amphibious}, swim Speed 20 feet; {@b Melee} current {@dice 1d20+7|+7} ({@trait water}), Damage {@dice 1d8+2} bludgeoning

{@b Positive Energy Plane (the Enlightened)} The enlightened appear as diaphanous, radiant versions of their mortal selves; {@b Alignment} any good; {@b Language} {@language Jyoti}; Additional Ability {@ability fast healing} 5; {@b Melee} glowing touch {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} positive

{@b Shadow Plane (the Mutilated)} The mutilated appear as they did in their mortal lives, yet are covered in wounds or partially adorned or wrapped in chains; {@b Alignment} any evil; {@b Language} {@language Shadowtongue}; {@b Additional Ability} immune to bleed and fear; {@b Melee} chain {@dice 1d20+7|+7}, Damage {@dice 1d8+2} bludgeoning

bot.name: Planar Incarnation

mid.trigger: A creature within 30 feet attempts a saving throw against piscovenom
entries: The creature takes an additional {@damage 2d8} poison damage even if it succeeds at its save.
mid.name: Enhance Venom

entries: {@damage 2d10+6} bludgeoning, {@dc 30}
bot.name: Constrict

entries: The piscodaemon makes two claw {@action Strike||Strikes} against the same creature. If both hit, the creature takes {@damage 2d10} {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed damage} and is exposed to piscovenom.
bot.name: Gory Rend

entries: 20 feet. Allies in the planetar's aura gain a +2 status bonus to AC against evil creatures and a +2 status bonus to damage rolls against evil creatures. The area in the aura is {@quickref difficult terrain||3|terrain} for evil creatures.
mid.name: Aura of Righteousness

entries: The planetar makes a greatsword {@action Strike} against a target it detects as evil. If the target is evil, the {@action Strike} deals three extra weapon damage dice and deals {@damage 1d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent good damage} to the target.
The planetar can convert all the physical damage from the attack into good damage.
bot.name: Blade of Justice

entries: The planetar can take on the appearance of any Small or Medium humanoid. This doesn't change their Speed or {@action Strike||Strikes}.
bot.name: Change Shape

entries: Any weapon gains the effect of a holy property rune while a planetar wields it.
bot.name: Holy Armaments

entries: The polar bear's {@action Strike||Strikes} deal an additional {@damage 1d6} precision damage to {@condition flat-footed} creatures.
bot.name: Sneak Attack

entries: The polar bear gains a +3 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against creatures it has {@condition grabbed}.
bot.name: Mauler

attacks.name: glaive
attacks.damage: {@damage 3d8+17} slashing plus {@damage 2d6} evil and {@damage 4d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed}

attacks.name: hurled weapon
attacks.damage: {@damage 2d10+12} piercing plus {@damage 1d6} evil and {@damage 4d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed}

entries: The purrodaemon causes a weapon that has steeped in their flesh (see Steep Weapon) to telekinetically launch from their flesh. The purrodaemon makes a hurled weapon {@action Strike} without using their hands.
bot.name: Hurl Weapon

bot.requirements: A steeped weapon that's no longer sheathed in the purrodaemon's body is within 120 feet of the daemon
entries: The steeped weapon swiftly flies through the air to resheath itself in the purrodaemon's body. If a creature is along this flight path, the purrodaemon can make a hurled weapon {@action Strike} against the target; if it hits, the weapon drops to the ground in a square adjacent to the creature.
bot.name: Recall Weapon

entries: The purrodaemon sheathes a weapon in their own flesh. This deals no damage to the purrodaemon, which can have up to 10 weapons sheathed in their body at a time. A steeped weapon must be one that deals piercing or slashing damage.

If a purrodaemon {@action Interact||Interacts} to crush a soul gem, one weapon of their choice sheathed in their flesh becomes enchanted with the daemon's fiendish power and becomes a +2 greater striking weapon that can be used in place of their glaive or hurled at targets (with a +2 item bonus to the hurled weapon's attack modifier and an extra d10 of damage). This magical quality fades 24 hours after it ceases being sheathed in the daemon's living body. A steeped weapon can be Disarmed.
bot.name: Steep Weapon

bot.trigger: The purrodaemon is hit with a weapon that deals piercing damage
bot.requirements: The purrodaemon has fewer than 10 weapons sheathed in their body
entries: The purrodaemon seizes the triggering weapon. The weapon's wielder must attempt a {@dc 40} Reflex save. On a failure, the weapon is disarmed and falls to an adjacent square. On a critical failure, the weapon is sheathed in the purrodaemon's body as though the daemon had used Steep Weapon. an ability. Cacodaemons (Pathfinder Bestiary 70) are the most common also be created by spells like bind soul. If a daemon crushes a soul gem.
bot.name: Twist the Blade

attacks.name: jaws
attacks.damage: {@damage 2d10+13} piercing plus quetz couatl venom and Grab

entries: {@damage 2d10+7} bludgeoning, {@dc 29}
bot.name: Greater Constrict
Quetz Couatl Venom
bot.note: A quetz couatl's venom deals good damage rather than poison damage to fiends

entries: The quetz couatl spreads its multicolored wings and radiant plumage. Each enemy within 30 feet must attempt a {@dc 29} Will save.

entries.Critical Success: The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Radiant Wings for 24 hours.
entries.Success: The creature is {@condition dazzled} for 1 round.
entries.Failure: The creature is {@condition dazzled} for 1 minute.
entries.Critical Failure: As failure, but if the creature is evil, it is also {@condition stunned 3}.

bot.name: Radiant Wings

bot.requirements: The quetz couatl has a
entries: Medium or smaller creature {@condition grabbed} or {@condition restrained} in its jaws; The quetz couatl moves the creature into its coils, freeing its fangs to make attacks, then uses.
bot.name: Wrap in Coils

entries: Constrict against the creature. The quetz couatl can hold as many creatures in its coils as will fit in its space.
bot.name: Greater

entries: The quickling takes a –2 status penalty to saving throws against effects that cause the {@condition slowed} condition. If the quickling ever becomes {@condition slowed}, they lose their supernatural speed, can't Fade from View, and become {@condition sickened 1} for the duration of the slow. They also can't reduce this {@condition sickened} condition for the duration of the {@condition slowed} effect.
mid.name: Slow Susceptibility

mid.trigger: The quickling is targeted by a {@action Strike}
entries: The quickling darts aside, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC, then {@action Stride||Strides} up to half their Speed after the {@action Strike} resolves.
mid.name: Can't Catch Me

entries: The quickling's {@action Strike||Strikes} deal an extra {@damage 1d6} precision damage to {@condition flat-footed} creatures.
bot.name: Sneak Attack

entries: The quickling's speed, combined with nearly instantaneous acceleration and deceleration, enables them to move in astonishing ways. As long as they have a firm surface to travel across, they can {@action Stride} their full movement vertically or horizontally. They can even run across unstable surfaces, such as water, in the same way, although dangerous surfaces (acid, lava, etc.) harm them as normal. They must end their movement on a horizontal surface capable of bearing their weight or else they fall.
bot.name: Supernatural Speed

entries: Tentacle only.
mid.name: Attack of Opportunity

bot.requirements: The quoppopak's ventral tube is in water
entries: The quoppopak uses Tentacle Stab against a creature within reach; if either of these {@action Strike||Strikes} hits, the creature must attempt a {@dc 30} Reflex save. On a failed save, the quoppopak shoots water through its tentacles into the creature, dealing {@damage 2d6+5} bludgeoning damage and making the creature {@condition sickened 1} as its internal organs fill with water (double damage and {@condition sickened 2} on a critical failure).
bot.name: Flooding Thrust

entries: The quoppopak makes two tentacle {@action Strike||Strikes} against the same creature. These {@action Strike||Strikes} deal piercing damage instead of bludgeoning, and the quoppopak can't Grab with them. Its multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks.
bot.name: Tentacle Stab

entries: The quoppopak can stand and move on the surface of water or other liquids without falling through. It can go underwater if it wishes, but it must {@action Swim} to do so.
bot.name: Water Glide

entries: 60 feet. A creature that uses a teleportation ability within the aura's emanation or enters it via a teleportation ability must succeed a {@dc 38} Will save or become {@condition sickened 1} and have its destination changed to a point of the radiant warden's choosing within the emanation. On a successful save, the creature arrives as intended but is still {@condition sickened 1}.
mid.name: Gatekeeper Aura

entries: Until it acts, the radiant warden appears to be an orrery (or similar large mechanical contraption, such as a telescope). It has an automatic result of 53 on {@skill Deception} checks and DCs to convincingly pass as such a machine.
bot.name: Orrery

entries: The radiant warden releases a 50-foot cone of bright energy that deals {@damage 10d12} force damage ({@dc 38} basic Reflex save). The radiant warden can't use Radiant Blast for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
bot.name: Radiant Blast

entries: When a creature is hit by the radiant warden's hammer {@action Strike}, a flash of radiant energy attempts to anchor the creature in place. The creature must attempt a {@dc 38} Will save; on a failure, the creature can't use any {@trait teleportation} effects for 1 minute.
On a critical failure, the creature is also permanently {@condition blinded}.
bot.name: Radiant Blow

entries: A raven can use simple items as tools, such as poking a stick at an opening to tease out a piece of food.

They are also quite adept at stealing objects. A raven can't use {@skill Thievery} to {@action Palm an Object}, {@action Disable a Device}, or {@action Pick a Lock}, but it can use {@skill Thievery} to {@action Steal} light objects that it can carry in its beak or talons or to accomplish other relatively simple tasks.
top.name: Cunning

entries: There are few things as dangerously persistent in the natural world as an angry unkindness of ravens. A raven swarm can hound its prey through most barriers. Simple latches, unsecured chimney flues, loosely shuttered windows, and similar obstacles rarely keep an unkindness away. A raven swarm attempts a {@skill Thievery} check to bypass many of these simple obstructions, typically against {@dc 20}.
bot.name: Enraged Cunning

entries: The ravens' angry pecking deals {@damage 1d8} piercing damage to each enemy in the swarm's space ({@dc 20} basic Reflex save). A creature that critically fails its save is {@condition blinded} for {@dice 1d4} rounds as the ravens focus their attacks on the target's vulnerable face.
bot.name: Swarming Beaks

entries: Smoke doesn't impair an ancient red ravener's vision; it ignores the {@condition concealed} condition from smoke.
top.name: Smoke Vision

entries: 90 feet, {@dc 42}
mid.name: Cowering Fear

entries: 10 feet, {@damage 4d6} fire damage ({@dc 41} basic Reflex save)
mid.name: Dragon Heat

entries: 200 HP. An intangible field of necromantic energy protects a ravener from total destruction. A soul ward has 150 maximum Hit Points, or 200 if the ravener is level 21 or higher. Whenever a ravener would be reduced below 1 Hit Point, all damage in excess of what would reduce them to 1 Hit Point is instead dealt to their soul ward. If this damage reduces the soul ward to fewer than 0 Hit Points, the ravener is destroyed. A soul ward's Hit Points can be restored only via specific ravener abilities such as Consume Soul, ravenous breath, or vicious criticals. A ravener who goes more than a week without successfully using Consume Soul to feed on a dying creature starves, and their soul ward loses {@damage 1d4} Hit Points each day until they feed. If the ravener's soul ward loses all its Hit Points while the ravener still has more than 1 HP, they become a ravener husk.
mid.name: Soul Ward

entries: Jaws only.
mid.name: Attack of Opportunity

mid.trigger: The ravener takes excess damage to their soul ward but still has at least 51 Hit Points in their soul ward;
entries: The ravener draws deeply into their soul ward, discorporating their body into soul energy in order to escape. They take 50 damage to their soul ward and their physical body vanishes, reappearing {@dice 1d4} hours later in a random location within 1 mile from the location where they used Discorporate.
mid.name: Discorporate

mid.trigger: A creature within 100 feet casts a fire spell, or a fire spell otherwise comes into effect from a source within 100 feet
entries: The ravener makes all the choices to determine the targets, destination, and other effects of the spell, as though they were the caster.
mid.name: Redirect Fire

bot.trigger: A living creature within 30 feet of the ravener dies
entries: The ravener tears the creature's soul from its body with their maw and gulps it down. The dying creature must attempt a {@dc 44} Fortitude save.

entries.Success: The ravener tears off a small chunk of the creature's soul. If the victim is restored to life, they are {@condition drained 1} in addition to any other side effects of returning to life. The ravener adds a number of Hit Points to their soul ward equal to half the creature's level.
entries.Failure: As success, but the creature's soul is ravaged. The creature is {@condition drained 3} and the ravener adds a number of Hit Points to their soul ward equal to the creature's level.
entries.Critical Failure: As failure, but the ravener devours the entire soul. The victim can't be restored to life as long as the ravener exists except via a 10th-level effect such as {@spell miracle} or {@spell wish}, and the ravener adds a number of Hit Points to their soul ward equal to twice the creature's level.

bot.name: Consume Soul

entries: The ravener attempts to take control of a magical fire or a fire spell within 100 feet. If it succeeds at a counteract check (counteract level 10, counteract modifier +34), the original caster loses control of the spell or magical fire, control is transferred to the ravener, and this action counts as the ravener having Sustained the Spell with the action (if applicable). The ravener can choose to end the spell instead of taking control, if they choose.
bot.name: Manipulate Flames

entries: The ravener breathes a blast of flame that deals {@damage 20d6} fire damage plus {@damage 4d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent negative damage} ({@dc 44} basic Reflex save). A creature that fails its save is also {@condition drained 1} (or {@condition drained 2} on a critical failure). If a creature is {@condition drained} by the ravener's Ravenous Breath Weapon, the ravener's soul ward gains 5 HP. The ravener can't use Breath Weapon again for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
bot.name: Ravenous Breath Weapon

entries: The ravener treats an attack roll as a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20, as long as the attack roll was a success. Additionally, whenever the ravener makes a critical hit with one of their {@action Strike|Strikes}, the target must succeed on a Fortitude save or gain the {@condition drained 1} condition. If the target already has a {@condition drained} value of greater than 0, their {@condition drained} value instead increases by 1, to a maximum of {@condition drained 4}. Whenever the ravener applies drain to a creature in this way, their soul ward gains 5 Hit Points.
bot.name: Vicious Criticals

entries: 90 feet, {@dc 31}
mid.name: Frightful Presence

mid.trigger: The ravener husk takes any amount of bludgeoning damage
entries: The ravener's brittle bones shatter, spraying bone shards everywhere. Every creature within a 10-foot emanation of the ravener husk takes {@damage 7d6} piercing damage ({@dc 31} basic Reflex save).
mid.name: Boneshatter

entries: The ravener husk breathes a torrent of negative energy that deals {@damage 16d6} negative damage in a 40-foot cone ({@dc 34} basic Reflex save). They can't use Breath Weapon again for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
bot.name: Breath Weapon

entries: The ravener husk makes a jaws {@action Strike} against a deceased creature that has been dead no longer than 1 minute, was good aligned, and was at least level 15 in life. The ravener attempts a DC {@flatDC 5} flat check; if successful, they transform back into a ravener with 1 Hit Point in their soul ward.
bot.name: Ravenous Repast

entries: The blue-ringed octopus can change the color of its skin to {@action Hide} even if it doesn't have cover.
bot.name: Camouflage

entries: The reef octopus emits a cloud of dark-brown ink in a 10-foot emanation. This cloud has no effect outside of water. Creatures inside the cloud are {@condition hidden} and can't use their sense of smell. The cloud dissipates after 1 minute. The octopus can't use Ink Cloud again for {@dice 2d6} rounds.
bot.name: Ink Cloud

entries: The reef octopus moves up to 80 feet in a straight line through the water without triggering reactions.
bot.name: Jet

entries: The reef octopus makes up to four arm {@action Strike||Strikes} with different arms, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the octopus's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it makes all of its attacks.
bot.name: Writhing Arms

notes: any one spoken in life by their murderer (typically {@language common})

entries: A revenant knows the direction of their murderer (as long as both are on the same plane), but not the distance.
top.name: Sense Murderer

entries: If a revenant sees their own reflection or any object that was important to them in life, they must attempt a {@dc 25} Will save.

entries.Critical Success: The revenant is unaffected and can no longer be affected by that reflection or object in this way.
entries.Success: The revenant is distracted by self-loathing and becomes {@condition slowed 1} for 1 round.
entries.Failure: The revenant becomes {@condition fascinated} by the source that triggered their self-loathing and does everything they can to destroy it until the end of the revenant's next turn.
entries.Critical Failure: The revenant becomes {@condition immobilized} as long as the source of their self-loathing is apparent, until they're attacked, or until they see their murderer.

mid.name: Self-Loathing

entries: If the revenant's murderer dies, the revenant is immediately destroyed. A revenant that can't sense their murderer must attempt a DC {@flatDC 11} flat check once every 24 hours to avoid becoming {@condition immobilized} and {@condition prone}; they immediately rise again once they can sense their murderer. A murderer who becomes undead does not trigger the revenant's destruction until the murderer is finally destroyed. The revenant gains a +2 status bonus to checks and DCs against their murderer.
mid.name: Undying Vendetta

entries: The revenant wails horribly. Each creature within a 60-foot burst must attempt a {@dc 23} Will save. Regardless of the outcome of their saving throw, affected creatures are then immune to Baleful Shriek for 1 hour. The revenant's murderer never improves their degree of success due to this ability's {@trait incapacitation} trait. The revenant can't use Baleful Shriek again for {@dice 1d4} rounds.

entries.Success: The creature is {@condition frightened 2}.
entries.Failure: The creature is {@condition frightened 2} and {@condition paralyzed} for 1 round.
entries.Critical Failure: The creature is {@condition frightened 3} and {@condition paralyzed} for {@dice 1d4} rounds.

bot.name: Baleful Shriek

entries: {@damage 2d6+5} bludgeoning, {@dc 24}
bot.name: Constrict

entries: The rhinoceros {@action Stride||Strides} twice, then makes a horn {@action Strike}. As long as the rhinoceros moved at least 20 feet, the {@action Strike}'s damage increases to {@dice 3d8+6}. A Medium or smaller creature struck by this attack must succeed at a {@dc 21} Reflex save or be automatically Shoved back 5 feet and knocked {@condition prone} by the force of the blow.
bot.name: Rhinoceros Charge

entries: Medium or smaller, foot, {@dc 18}
bot.name: Trample

entries: A rusalka is {@condition concealed} while underwater.
mid.name: Blurred Form

entries: The rusalka cries out a compelling invitation. Each non-fey creature within a 300-foot emanation must attempt a {@dc 27} Will save. The effect lasts for 1 round, but if the rusalka uses Beckoning Call again on subsequent rounds, the duration extends by 1 round for all affected creatures. Once a creature succeeds at any save against Beckoning Call, that creature is temporarily immune for 24 hours.

entries.Success: The creature is unaffected.
entries.Failure: The creature is {@condition fascinated} and must spend each of its actions to move closer to the rusalka, avoiding obvious dangers. If a beckoned creature is adjacent to the rusalka, it stays still and doesn't act. If attacked by the rusalka, the creature is freed from captivation at the end of the rusalka's turn.
entries.Critical Failure: As failure, but if attacked by the rusalka, the creature can attempt a new save only at the start of its next turn, rather than being freed at the end of the rusalka's turn.

bot.name: Beckoning Call

entries: {@damage 2d8+10} bludgeoning, {@dc 32}
bot.name: Constrict

entries: A rusalka can have up to eight creatures {@condition grabbed} within their tresses at a time.
bot.name: Entangling Tresses

entries: The rusalka attempts an {@skill Athletics} check against each {@condition grabbed} creature's Fortitude DC. The rusalka moves each creature they succeed against up to 10 feet and each creature they critically succeed against up to 20 feet. This movement must all be within reach of its tresses.
bot.name: Flowing Hair

entries: The rusalka touches a creature within 5 feet using their hand, stirring up memories of regret and shame. The target must attempt a {@dc 35} Will save.

entries.Success: The target is {@condition sickened 1}.
entries.Failure: The creature is {@condition sickened 1} and {@condition stunned 1}.
entries.Critical Failure: The creature is {@condition sickened 1} and {@condition stunned 1}, and it must use its first action on its next turn to {@action Strike} itself, automatically hitting.

bot.name: Shameful Touch

attacks.name: barbed chain
attacks.damage: {@damage 2d8+9} piercing plus {@damage 1d6} evil and {@damage 2d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed}

entries: A velstrac automatically knows whether a creature it sees has any of the {@condition doomed}, {@condition dying}, and {@condition wounded} conditions, as well as the value of those conditions.
top.name: Painsight

entries: 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it sees a vision of the sacristan groveling in pitiable servitude. The creature must succeed at a {@dc 27} Will save or become {@condition stunned 1}.
mid.name: Staggering Servitude

entries: The sacristan stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The creature must immediately attempt a Will save against staggering servitude. In addition, if the creature was already {@condition stunned}, on a failed save its revulsion toward the sacristan's presence causes it to be {@condition stupefied 2} for 1 minute. After attempting this save, the creature is then temporarily immune until the start of the sacristan's next turn.
bot.name: Focus Gaze

entries: The sacristan opens their mouth to unloose the wailing howls and mind-twisting darkness of the Shadow Plane. This creates a 30-foot emanation of darkness and wailing sounds around the sacristan. Creatures with {@ability darkvision} can't see through this darkness. The sacristan can Sustain Shadow Scream for up to 1 minute. Non-velstrac creatures in the area when the ability is used, as well as those who enter or start their turn in the area, must attempt a {@dc 28} Will save.

entries.Critical Success: The creature is unaffected and is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.
entries.Success: The creature is {@condition deafened} for 1 round.
entries.Failure: The creature is {@condition confused} and {@condition deafened} for 1 round.
entries.Critical Failure: The creature takes 20 mental damage, and is {@condition confused} and {@condition deafened} for 1 round.

bot.name: Shadow Scream

entries: When the sand sentry takes fire or lightning damage, its outer layer of sand fuses into sheets of hardened glass for 1 minute. This increases the sand sentry's AC to 26 and grants it resistance 5 to acid, cold, electricity, fire, force, piercing, and slashing damage. A sand sentry can't use earth glide while glass armor is active.
mid.name: Glass Armor

entries: A living boulder can {@action Burrow} through earthen matter, including rock.
When it does so, it moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.
bot.name: Earth Glide

entries: When the sand sentry critically hits with a fist {@action Strike}, the target is {@condition blinded} for 1 round.
bot.name: Blinding Sand

entries: Hoof only.
mid.name: Attack of Opportunity

entries: The Sandpoint Devil breathes a 30-foot cone of flame that deals {@damage 6d10} fire damage. Each creature in the area must attempt a {@dc 26} Reflex save. The.
bot.name: Accursed Breath

entries: Devil can't use Accursed Breath for {@dice 1d4} rounds.

entries.Success: The creature takes half damage.
entries.Failure: The creature takes full damage and is {@condition sickened 2} by the agonizing pain of its burns.
entries.Critical Failure: The creature takes full damage and is cursed. The victim's flesh appears charred and burned, and the pain of being burned alive never fully vanishes. The cursed creature takes a –2 status penalty to all checks from the constant pain. Healing doesn't alter the appearance of the burns or lessen the penalty, but removing the curse does.

bot.name: Sandpoint

entries: The Sandpoint Devil unleashes a bloodcurdling howl that can be heard for miles. Each creature within a 100-foot emanation must succeed at a {@dc 26} Will save or become {@condition frightened 2} ({@condition frightened 3} and {@condition fleeing} as long as it remains {@condition frightened} on a critical failure). Creatures that attempt this Will save are then immune to Devil's Howl for 24 hours.
bot.name: Devil's Howl

entries: Medium or smaller, hoof, {@dc 23}
bot.name: Trample

entries: The sard treats the plane it is located on as its home plane.
top.name: Planar Acclimation

entries: When the sard dies, its body explodes in a 30-foot emanation.

All creatures in the area take {@damage 10d6} electricity damage and {@damage 10d6} piercing damage ({@dc 43} basic Reflex save). Any creature that takes piercing damage is also exposed to sard venom.
mid.name: Splintering Death

bot.trigger: The sard is about to damage a creature that has electricity resistance
entries: An instant before the target takes the electricity damage from the triggering event, the sard's electrical sparks glow red. The target must succeed at a {@dc 41} Will save or lose any electricity resistance it has until this curse is lifted.
bot.name: Lightning-Struck Curse

stages.entry: {@damage 2d6} electricity damage, {@damage 2d6} poison damage, and {@condition clumsy 2}
{@damage 3d6} electricity damage, {@damage 3d6} poison damage, {@condition clumsy 2}, and {@condition slowed 1}
{@damage 4d6} electricity damage, {@damage 4d6} poison damage, {@condition clumsy 2}, and {@condition slowed 2}

entries: The sard makes up to four thorn {@action Strike||Strikes}, each against a different target. The sard's multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks have been made.
bot.name: Thorn Volley

entries: Huge or smaller, root, {@dc 39}
bot.name: Trample

attacks.name: tentacle arm
attacks.damage: {@damage 2d8+9} bludgeoning plus {@damage 1d6} evil and sarglagon venom

entries: 10 feet. A creature that enters the heavy aura must attempt a {@dc 23} Will save. It is then temporarily immune for 10 minutes.

entries.Success: The creature is unaffected.
entries.Failure: The creature is {@condition encumbered} while it remains in the area. If the creature is already {@condition encumbered}, it is {@condition immobilized} while it remains within the aura.
entries.Critical Failure: As failure, but the effect persists for 3 rounds after leaving the aura.

mid.name: Heavy Aura

mid.trigger: A creature or object within the sarglagon's reach is targeted by an attack
entries: The sarglagon interposes themself, giving the creature or object standard cover against the attack (+2 circumstance bonus to AC), or greater cover (+4 circumstance bonus to AC) if the sarglagon was already granting it lesser cover.
mid.name: Stygian Guardian

entries: The sarglagon conjures murky water to fill the lungs of a creature that can't breathe water within 30 feet. The target must attempt a {@dc 26} Fortitude save.

entries.Success: The target coughs up water and is {@condition sickened 1}.
entries.Failure: The target is holding its breath (Core Rulebook 478). The only action it can take is to attempt a Fortitude save against Drown to expel the water, which is a single action.
entries.Critical Failure: The target falls {@condition unconscious} and begins suffocating. If the target succeeds at its Fortitude save while suffocating, it coughs up the water and can breathe again.

bot.name: Drown

entries: 40 feet. The scarecrow's eyes flicker with an unnerving glow. A creature can't reduce its {@condition frightened} condition below 1 as long as it is in the aura's emanation.
When a creature enters or starts its turn in the aura, it must attempt a {@dc 18} Will save. Birds and other avian creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to this save.

entries.Critical Success: The creature is unaffected and is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.
entries.Success: The creature is {@condition frightened 1}.
entries.Failure: The creature is {@condition frightened 2} and is {@condition fascinated} by the scarecrow until the end of its next turn.
entries.Critical Failure: As failure, but {@condition frightened 3}.

mid.name: Scarecrow's Leer

entries: The scarecrow's head bursts into ghostly, heatless flame that sheds bright light in a 20-foot emanation (and dim light to the next 20 feet). If the scarecrow uses this ability on the first round of combat, any creature that has not acted yet is startled and becomes {@condition flat-footed} against the scarecrow for 1 round. It can suppress the light by using this action again.
bot.name: Baleful Glow

entries: The scarecrow's strikes deal an additional {@damage 1d6} mental damage to {@condition frightened} creatures.
bot.name: Clawing Fear

entries: Until it acts, the scarecrow resembles an ordinary scarecrow. It has an automatic result of 32 on {@skill Deception} checks and DCs to pass as an ordinary scarecrow.
bot.name: Mundane Appearance

attacks.name: jaws
attacks.damage: {@damage 2d6+4} piercing plus {@damage 2d6} negative and drain life

entries: Sceaduinars have neither souls nor the ability to create. A sceaduinar is immune to effects that target a soul (such as bind soul or resurrect) or that require knowledge of a creature's identity (such as scrying), and critically fails {@skill Crafting} checks.
mid.name: Void Child

mid.trigger: A creature attempts a melee attack against a sceaduinar or an
entries: {@skill Acrobatics} check to {@action Tumble Through} the sceaduinar's space; The sceaduinar flexes its wings to emit a brief pulse of negative energy that deals {@damage 4d6} negative damage to the triggering creature ({@dc 22} basic Reflex save).
mid.name: Wing Flash

entries: When the sceaduinar damages a living creature with its jaws {@action Strike}, the sceaduinar gains 5 temporary Hit Points and the creature must succeed at a {@dc 25} Fortitude save or become {@condition drained 1}. Further damage dealt to the creature by the sceaduinar increases the {@condition drained} value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of {@condition drained 4}.
bot.name: Drain Life

entries: Negative damage dealt by a sceaduinar damages undead and creatures with negative healing as if it were positive damage.
The sceaduinar's melee {@action Strike||Strikes} have the benefits of the {@item ghost touch} property rune on attacks against incorporeal undead.
bot.name: Entropic Touch

entries: A scythe tree takes 5 additional damage from axes.
mid.name: Axe Vulnerability

mid.trigger: A creature rolls a critical failure on a melee weapon {@action Strike} against the scythe tree
entries: The scythe tree attempts to {@action Disarm} the creature.
mid.name: Ripping Disarm

entries: Until the scythe tree acts, it appears to be a dead tree. It has an automatic result of 35 on {@skill Deception} checks and DCs to pass as a dead tree.
bot.name: Dead Tree

bot.requirements: The scythe tree is using Dead Tree in forested terrain, and a creature that hasn't detected it is within 30 feet
entries: The scythe tree {@action Stride||Strides} up to 25 feet toward the triggering creature. Once the creature is in reach, the scythe tree makes a scythe branch {@action Strike} against it. The creature is {@condition flat-footed} against this {@action Strike}.
bot.name: Woodland Ambush

mid.trigger: An adjacent creature deals piercing or slashing damage to the sea drake
entries: An arc of electricity courses through the sea drake's blood. The triggering creature takes {@damage 1d6} electricity damage.
mid.name: Electrified Blood

entries: The sea drake spews a ball of electricity that strikes a primary target within 100 feet, dealing {@damage 7d6} electricity damage ({@dc 24} basic Reflex save). The lightning then arcs to up to three secondary targets within 30 feet of the primary target, striking the closest available targets first. The secondary bolts each strike one secondary target and deal the same rolled damage value as the primary bolt ({@dc 22} basic Reflex save). The sea drake can't use Ball Lightning Breath again for {@dice 1d6} rounds.
bot.name: Ball Lightning Breath

entries: A sea drake's saliva carries a large quantity of salt, making its bite wounds even more painful. When a creature takes damage from a sea drake's jaws {@action Strike}, the creature must attempt a {@dc 24} Fortitude save; the creature is then temporarily immune to briny wound for 1 minute.

entries.Success: The creature is {@condition sickened 1}.
entries.Failure: The creature is {@condition sickened 2}.
entries.Critical Failure: The creature is {@condition sickened 2} and {@condition slowed 1} as long as it's {@condition sickened}.

bot.name: Briny Wound

entries: The drake tries to capsize an adjacent aquatic vessel of its size or smaller. The drake must succeed at an {@skill Athletics} check with a DC of 25 (reduced by 5 for each size smaller the vessel is than the drake) or the pilot's {@skill Lore||Sailing Lore} DC, whichever is higher.
bot.name: Capsize

entries: The sea drake makes one jaws {@action Strike} and two tail {@action Strike||Strikes} in any order.
bot.name: Draconic Frenzy
Speed Surge
entries: The sea drake {@action Stride||Strides} or {@action Fly||Flies} twice.
The sea snake can hold its breath for about an hour.
top.name: Deep Breath

mid.trigger: A creature within the sea snake's reach uses a move action
entries: The sea snake makes a bite {@action Strike} against the attacker.
mid.name: Lash Out

entries: The shadow drake's form shifts and blends reflexively with surrounding shadows. A shadow drake gains an additional reaction each round, but it can use this reaction only for Shadow Evade.
top.name: Shadow Blend

top.trigger: A creature attacks the shadow drake while it is in an area of dim light
entries: The shadow drake further obscures its position. The attacker must succeed at a DC {@flatDC 11} flat check in order to affect the shadow drake, as if the drake were {@condition Hidden} for the triggering attack.
top.name: Shadow Evade

entries: The shadow drake makes one bite {@action Strike} and two tail {@action Strike||Strikes} in any order.
bot.name: Draconic Frenzy

entries: A shadow drake spits a ball of black liquid that explodes into a cloud of frigid black shadow. This attack has a range of 40 feet and explodes in a 5-foot-radius burst.

Creatures within the burst take {@damage 3d6} cold damage ({@dc 18} basic Reflex save). The explosion of shadow also snuffs out mundane light sources the size of a torch, lantern, or smaller, and attempts to counteract magical light with a +10 counteract modifier. The shadow drake can't use Shadow Breath again for {@dice 1d6} rounds.
bot.name: Shadow Breath
Speed Surge
entries: The shadow drake {@action Stride||Strides} or {@action Fly||Flies} twice.

When a shadow giant hits with a melee {@action Strike}, the target must succeed at a {@dc 30} Fortitude save or become {@condition drained 1} and take a –1 status penalty to {@skill Perception} checks involving sight as long as they remain {@condition drained}. On a critical failure, this condition doesn't heal naturally and can be removed only with magic.
bot.name: Pall of Shadow

entries: The shadow giant becomes shrouded in shadows and becomes {@condition concealed}. As the nature of this effect leaves the giant's location obvious, it can't use this concealment to {@action Hide} or {@action Sneak}. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until it is exposed to direct sunlight, whichever comes first.
bot.name: Shadowcloak

entries: When a creature slays a shoal linnorm, it must succeed at a {@dc 38} Will save or become cursed. As long as the curse of drowning persists, the character must spend 3 actions when drinking any liquid.
Attempting to drink liquid faster causes the victim to begin drowning, immediately running out of air and falling {@condition unconscious}.
mid.name: Curse of Drowning

entries: Tail only.
mid.name: Attack of Opportunity

entries: The shoal linnorm exhales scalding steam that deals {@damage 12d8} fire damage in a 60-foot cone ({@dc 36} basic Reflex save). The steam lingers until the end of the linnorm's next turn; anyone who enters the area or begins their turn in the area takes {@damage 6d8} fire damage ({@dc 36} Reflex save negates)

During this time, the steam is so thick that it impedes movement as well as sight, turning the area into {@quickref difficult terrain||3|terrain}. All creatures in the steam become {@condition concealed}, and all creatures outside the steam become {@condition concealed} to creatures within it. The shoal linnorm can't use Breath Weapon again for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
bot.name: Breath Weapon

entries: A shocker lizard can sense minute electrical charges in living creatures, which it can use as a precise sense at a range of 20 feet. This distance increases to 100 feet against any creature that has used an {@trait electricity} effect within the last minute.
top.name: Greater Electrolocation

bot.trigger: A shocker lizard within 20 feet makes a shock {@action Strike} or uses Discharge
entries: The shocker lizard lends some of its electrical power to the nearby lizard. This either causes the other lizard's shock {@action Strike} to lose the {@trait nonlethal} trait or increases the DC of its Discharge to 20.
bot.name: Amplify Voltage

entries: The shocker lizard releases a blast of lightning from its body. Creatures within a 10-foot emanation take {@damage 3d6} electricity damage ({@dc 18} basic Reflex save). The shocker lizard can't use Discharge for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
bot.name: Discharge

entries: The shocker lizard hunkers down and begins to spark with electricity, which it releases in a 10-foot emanation that deals {@damage 3d8} electricity damage ({@dc 18} basic Reflex save). The shocker lizard can't use.
bot.name: Shocking Burst

entries: Burst again for 1 minute.

Up to four shocker lizards can combine their Shocking Bursts. In this case, the lizards can delay the release of their electricity until any other shocker lizard within 30 feet completes its Shocking Burst. The combined Shocking Bursts create an emanation with the combined area from all participating shocker lizards. For each additional shocker lizard that joins the burst, the damage increases by {@damage 3d8} and the save DC increases by 1 (to a maximum of four lizards dealing {@dice 12d8} damage with a {@dc 21} save).
bot.name: Shocking

entries: The shoggti caresses a creature with a tentacle and supernaturally erodes the creature's capability for thought as it does so. The target must succeed at a {@dc 25} Will save or become {@condition stupefied 2}, with an unlimited duration. The target can attempt the save to remove the {@condition stupefied} condition again once per day when it makes its daily preparations.
bot.name: Braincloud

entries: {@damage 1d10+8} bludgeoning, {@dc 25}
bot.name: Constrict

entries: The shoggti writhes its tentacles and shifts its coloration to put on an unnaturally fascinating hypnotic display. Creatures in a 30-foot emanation must attempt a {@dc 25} Will save, after which they are temporarily immune to further Fascinating Displays for 1 minute.

entries.Success: The creature is {@condition sickened 1}.
entries.Failure: The creature is {@condition fascinated} for 1 minute. Once the fascination effect ends, the creature is {@condition sickened 1}.
entries.Critical Failure: The creature is {@condition fascinated} for 1 minute, can't use reactions while {@condition fascinated}, and is {@condition sickened 2} once the fascination ends.

bot.name: Fascinating Display

entries: A skaveling can use its hearing as a precise sense at the listed range.
top.name: Echolocation

entries: The skaveling unleashes a horrifying screech that chills the very bones of those close enough to feel it.
The screech can be heard for miles, but each creature in a 20-foot emanation must also attempt a {@dc 22} Will save. The skaveling can't use Bone-Chilling Screech again for {@dice 1d4} rounds.

entries.Critical Success: The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Bone-

bot.name: Bone-Chilling Screech

entries: Screech for 24 hours.

entries.Success: The creature is {@condition frightened 1}.
entries.Failure: The creature is {@condition frightened 2}.
entries.Critical Failure: The creature is {@condition frightened 2} and {@condition stunned 1} by fear.

bot.name: Chilling

stages.entry: {@damage 2d6} negative damage and regains half as many Hit Points from all healing
{@damage 2d6} negative damage and gains no benefit from healing

entries: Any creature hit by the skaveling's {@action Strike||Strikes} must attempt a {@dc 22} Fortitude save.

entries.Success: The creature is {@condition slowed 1}.
entries.Failure: The creature is {@condition paralyzed}. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each save. into a standard potion of flying.

bot.name: Paralysis
